headline: First-ever evidence-based baseline (Gen-2 forensic anchor #6 / Night 8): $1.675B EPA Clean Air Act civil penalty (Jan 2024) — largest in CAA history and second-largest environmental penalty ever — plus April 16 Law360 federal sex-bias suit survival; uniform 4×7+3.5×1=81.4 cluster invalidated for the sixth time
methodology_notes: F500 Gen-2 cluster 4×7+3.5×1=81.4 — sixth consecutive forensic anchor invalidating this cluster (TIAA Apr 23 -38.9, Masimo Apr 23 -33.0, BD Apr 24 -27.3, Danaher Apr 25 -26.7, General Mills Apr 25 -28.3, Eli Lilly Apr 25 -25.1). Cummins delta of -28.3 is consistent with the General Mills calibration (also -28.3). Distinct profile from prior anchors: INT drops to 31.3 — the lowest INT score in the Gen-2 forensic series — because the EPA defeat-device scheme involved deliberate engineering of software to deactivate emission controls during normal driving, a multi-year affirmative deception affecting ~1 million vehicles. This is categorically more severe on the integrity dimension than Danaher's misleading-statements pattern or Eli Lilly's qui-tam petition. BND drops to 37.5: deliberate emissions fraud disproportionately raises NOx exposure in already-overburdened communities adjacent to highway corridors, a population-level boundary violation comparable to BD's environmental profile. ACT 62.5 retained: Cummins' announced zero-emission and hydrogen pivots, plus Accelera segment investments, are documented mission infrastructure. SYS 62.5 retained on global engine manufacturing supplying transit, agriculture, and emergency-power generation. EQU 50.0 reflects both the pollution-equity dimension and the Apr 16 Green v. Cummins federal sex-bias case which survived motion to dismiss in NC federal court. ACC 50.0 — settlement was paid in full but EPA filings document repeated prior episodes of recall non-compliance across 2007/2010/2013, indicating accountability is reactive rather than proactive. Cluster-artifact invalidation is now established beyond any reasonable doubt; this proposal completes the forensic series for the 81.4 cluster's industrial-sector entity. Band change Exemplary → Functional consistent with calibration.

evidence.claim: Cummins agreed January 10, 2024 to pay a record $1.675B civil penalty to the U.S. and California for installing illegal software defeat devices on 630,000 model-year 2013-2019 RAM 2500/3500 diesel trucks (~1M vehicles total 2013-2023). Largest civil penalty in Clean Air Act history; second-largest environmental penalty in U.S. history. Total estimated cost approaches $2B including recall and mitigation.

Defeat devices reduced or deactivated emission controls during normal driving, raising real-world NOx output above certified levels. Recall must be completed for at least 85% of vehicles within three years or trigger additional penalties. EPA enforcement page memorializes settlement terms.
California Attorney General Bonta and CARB announced separate $372M settlement under state law claims; CalMatters confirms record nature of joint settlement and harm framing.

Law360 Apr 16, 2026: North Carolina federal judge denied Cummins' motion to dismiss in Green v. Cummins — male office technician's Title VII sex-discrimination suit alleging he was denied training and stuck with entry-level tasks for years while female colleagues were advanced — case survives to discovery.

Mission infrastructure: Cummins' Accelera by Cummins segment (zero-emission hydrogen and battery-electric powertrains), Destination Zero strategy, and global supply of engines for transit, agriculture, and emergency-power generation supports SYS 62.5 and ACT 62.5 floor — genuine ongoing positive contribution to suffering reduction infrastructure.

Published 81.4 is uniform 4×7+3.5×1 Gen-2 cluster profile shared with American Express, Danaher, Eli Lilly, General Mills, U.S. Bancorp at F500 mid-ranks — invalidated as evidence-based by six consecutive forensic anchors (TIAA Apr 23, Masimo Apr 23, BD Apr 24, Danaher Apr 25, General Mills Apr 25, Eli Lilly Apr 25).

key_evidence: $1.675B EPA Clean Air Act civil penalty (Jan 2024) — largest in CAA history, second-largest environmental penalty ever; ~1M vehicles affected with deliberate defeat-device software
Total estimated cost ~$2B including recall and mitigation; California $372M state-law settlement on top of federal
Apr 16 Green v. Cummins federal sex-discrimination suit survives motion to dismiss (Title VII)
Genuine zero-emission pivot (Accelera, Destination Zero) supports SYS/ACT 62.5 floor
Published 81.4 is uniform Gen-2 cluster profile invalidated by 6 prior forensic anchors
INT 31.3 is the lowest of the Gen-2 anchor series — multi-year deliberate engineering deception is more severe than misleading-statements or petition-to-dismantle patterns
